title_ar: مقدمة ابن الصلاح — الموقوف والمقطوع والمسلسل
content_en:
The category of Hasan (good/fair) hadith is one of the most important and at the same time most debated classifications in hadith science. Ibn al-Salah's Muqaddimah gives it extended treatment, acknowledging that the boundaries between Hasan and adjacent categories — the higher grade of Sahih and the lower grade of Da'if — have been a subject of significant scholarly discussion.

Ibn al-Salah acknowledges that the term 'hasan' was used by the great hadith scholar al-Tirmidhi (209–279 AH) in his Sunan with great frequency, often in formulations like 'hadha hadithun hasanun' or 'hasanun gharibun' — but al-Tirmidhi himself did not provide a systematic definition of what he meant by 'hasan.' This left later scholars to reconstruct al-Tirmidhi's criteria from his practice, a task that generated considerable scholarly discussion.

Ibn al-Salah's own formulation is that the Hasan hadith is one that meets all the conditions of a Sahih hadith except that one or more of its narrators has a degree of weakness in their precision (dabt) — not so severe that the narration must be rejected entirely, but sufficient that it falls short of the highest standard. In practice, this often means a narrator who is described with terms like 'saduq' (truthful), 'la ba'sa bihi' (no objection to him), or 'yasluh fi al-shawahid' (acceptable as a supporting narration).

A critical contribution of Ibn al-Salah's chapter on Hasan is his recognition that a Da'if (weak) narration can be elevated to Hasan status if it is supported by other chains of transmission that corroborate its content. When multiple weak chains, each with a different type of weakness, converge to support the same textual content, they together provide a degree of corroboration that can elevate the narration's status. This is the concept of al-hadith al-hasan li-ghayrihi (a narration that is 'hasan' because of external supporting evidence) — distinguished from al-hadith al-hasan li-dhatihi (intrinsically hasan, because its own chain, while not reaching the level of sahih, is not severely weak).

The legal significance of the Hasan hadith is important: like the Sahih, the Hasan hadith is legally binding and actionable. All four major schools of Islamic jurisprudence accept Hasan hadiths as establishing religious obligations and prohibitions. This is a matter of consensus among the fuqaha (jurists). Where Hasan and Sahih differ is in the degree of certainty attached to them: a Sahih narration produces near-certain knowledge (ilm al-ghalaba wa al-zann al-muqarrab min al-yaqin), while a Hasan narration produces a somewhat lower level of probabilistic certainty — still sufficient for legal action.

The great hadith scholars whose collections are the primary sources of Hasan narrations — al-Tirmidhi, Abu Dawud, al-Nasa'i, and Ibn Majah — applied more inclusive criteria than al-Bukhari and Muslim, and their collections accordingly contain a mix of Sahih and Hasan narrations, along with some Da'if, the weakness of which they often noted. Working with these collections therefore requires greater critical judgment from the hadith scholar than working with the Sahihayn.
content_id:
Kategori Hasan (baik/cukup baik) adalah salah satu klasifikasi terpenting sekaligus paling diperdebatkan dalam ilmu hadis. Muqaddimah Ibn ash-Shalah memberikan pembahasan yang panjang tentangnya, mengakui bahwa batasan antara Hasan dan kategori-kategori yang berdekatan — kelas Shahih yang lebih tinggi dan Dha'if yang lebih rendah — memang tidak selalu jelas.

Ibn ash-Shalah mendefinisikan hadis Hasan sebagai hadis yang sanadnya bersambung melalui perawi yang adil tetapi sedikit kurang kuat hafalannya (khafif ad-dhabt), terbebas dari syudzudz dan illah.

Diskusi tentang hadis Hasan menjadi semakin kompleks karena istilah ini digunakan secara berbeda oleh para imam hadis yang berbeda:

Imam at-Tirmidzi sering menggunakan istilah "hasan shahih" atau "hasan gharib" yang tidak selalu bermakna sama dengan definisi standar yang kemudian dirumuskan oleh Ibn ash-Shalah.

Imam al-Bukhari dan Muslim tidak secara eksplisit menggunakan kategorisasi hasan dalam koleksi mereka, meskipun para ulama kemudian mengidentifikasi hadis-hadis yang setingkat hasan dalam koleksi selain keduanya.

Praktis, para ulama menggunakan hadis hasan sebagai hujjah (dalil) dalam fiqih, meskipun dengan derajat kehati-hatian yang lebih dibandingkan hadis shahih. Ini berbeda dengan hadis dha'if yang tidak dapat dijadikan hujjah dalam menentukan hukum syariat.

Perbedaan penting lainnya adalah antara hasan li dzatihi (hasan karena dirinya sendiri) dan hasan li ghayrihi (hasan karena ada dukungan dari riwayat lain). Yang kedua adalah hadis yang pada awalnya dha'if ringan tetapi naik derajatnya karena diperkuat oleh jalur periwayatan lain.
content_ar:
في كتاب "مقدمة ابن الصلاح" في علوم الحديث، يتناول المؤلف في هذا الجزء نوعًا من أنواع علوم الحديث النبوي الشريف مما له صلة بالموضوع المعنون أعلاه، مستعينًا بالأدلة الشرعية من الكتاب والسنة وأقوال الأئمة والعلماء.

ويُفصّل المؤلف في هذا القسم المسائل الواردة تفصيلًا وافيًا، مع الإشارة إلى وجوه الخلاف بين العلماء ومواضع الاتفاق، مُستندًا إلى منهج أهل العلم في التحقيق والتدقيق والاستدلال بالدليل الصحيح.

ويُركّز المؤلف على استيعاب الفروع الفقهية والمسائل العلمية المتعلقة بهذا الموضوع، موضّحًا الحِكَم والعلل الشرعية حيث أمكن، مما يُعين القارئ على الفهم الصحيح والتطبيق السليم لما يتعلمه من أحكام ومسائل.

ويختتم هذا الجزء بخلاصة جامعة للمسائل التي تناولها المؤلف، مع الإشارة إلى المراجع والمصادر التي استقى منها معلوماته، وهو ما يُعزز الثقة بمضمون الكتاب ويجعله مرجعًا موثوقًا في بابه.
